name: A167 DURHAM WESTERN BYPASS 1/12
units: metres below ground level, transcribed verbatim from a typed 1:25 Wimpey Geotech log; depth = base of interval; OD levels cross-checked as 59.75 - depth (all consistent, no clerk slips)

intervals.verbatim: TOPSOIL
Loose brown clayey silty fine SAND with some rootlets
Dark brown clayey silty fine SAND with some rootlets
Medium dense and dense brown clayey silty fine and medium SAND and fine to coarse angular to subrounded GRAVEL
annotations: pencil marginalia (sheet 1): 'Founding level ?' with an arrow near ~2.8m; an engineering foundation note, NOT a seam correlation
water: first ground-water seepage encountered at 2.60m, level rose 0.90m in 15 min (GWe 2.80m in water column); cut off by casing at 4.40m (GWc 4.40m)

intervals.verbatim: Stiff laminated greyish brown silty CLAY with occasional silty fine and fine and medium sand pockets and occasional reddish brown silty clay laminations:occasional disturbed lamination
annotations: sheet-2 continuation header prints this bed as 'Firm laminated greyish brown silty CLAY...'; consistency term differs from sheet-1 'Stiff'. Single bed, one boundary, base 5.90m

intervals.verbatim: Firm and stiff laminated brown silty CLAY with occasional fine and medium gravel of coal and occasional brown silt partings
Stiff laminated brown silty CLAY with occasional silt partings and some pockets of orange brown silty fine and medium sand with some fine and medium angular to subrounded gravel
annotations: water: second ground-water seepage encountered at 9.35m, level rose 1.95m in 15 min (GWe 9.35m); cut off at 14.60m by casing (GWc 14.60m)

intervals.verbatim: Brown sandy silty CLAY with some fine to coarse angular to subrounded bands of orange brown silty fine and medium sand with some fine to coarse angular to subrounded gravel and occasional gravel of coal:driller noted some bands of laminated silty clay
Stiff becoming very stiff below 16.30m brown sandy silty CLAY with some fine to coarse angular to subrounded gravel and occasional cobbles and occasional fine sand pocket
annotations: water: third ground-water seepage encountered at 19.35m, level rose 1.05m in 15 min (GWe 19.35m; water-depth readings 19.05m / 15.20m recorded in the water column)

intervals.verbatim: Stiff brown with occasional grey mottles sandy silty CLAY with some fine to coarse angular to subrounded gravel
Grey SILTSTONE
annotations: weathered rockhead; logged simply as 'Grey SILTSTONE' at the base of cable percussion (Cable Percussion to 25.60m; Rotary Core Drilling below). Detailed cored descriptions begin at 25.60m

intervals.verbatim: Grey highly weathered SILTSTONE-weak.Highly fractured.Much orange brown iron-staining on fracture surfaces.Some carbonaceous material
Grey slightly weathered SILTSTONE-moderately strong.Closely spaced fractures.Some carbonaceous material.
Grey highly weathered SILTSTONE-weak and moderately weak.Highly fractured.Some carbonaceous material.

Grey laminated slightly weathered SILTSTONE-moderately weak.Highly fractured.Occasional pale grey fine grained sandstone laminations.Very weak band 26.93m to 26.95m;27.20m to 27.25m and 28.20m to 28.35m.Some carbonaceous material.

Grey laminated slightly weathered SILTSTONE-moderately strong.Very closely and closely spaced fractures.Some carbonaceous material.Occasional pale grey fine grained sandstone laminations and bands.Occasional weak band from 29.20m to 29.21m;29.30m to 29.32m;29.63m to 29.67m and 29.78m to 29.85m

Pale grey fine grained bedded slightly weathered SANDSTONE-moderately strong and strong.Very closely to medium spaced fractures.Some dark grey siltstone laminations and bands.Very weak band from 31.11m to 31.12m

Pale grey fine grained bedded fresh SANDSTONE-strong.Very closely to medium spaced fractures,locally extremely closely spaced fractures.Occasional dark grey siltstone lamination.Occasional micaceous lamination.Highly fractured and weak from 32.54m to 32.58m and 34.66m to 34.68m.Weak siltstone band from 35.36m to 35.39m
Interbedded pale grey fine grained fresh SANDSTONE-strong and dark grey faintly weathered SILTSTONE-moderately weak.Highly fractured
annotations: interbedded sandstone + siltstone; classed sandstone (lead lithology). Immediately overlies the coal roof mudstone

intervals.verbatim: Black laminated MUDSTONE-moderately weak.Highly fractured
annotations: immediate roof of the coal seam below

intervals.verbatim: Black fresh COAL-moderately weak.Highly fractured
annotations: 1.10m coal seam at 35.55-36.65m (approx. 24.20 to 23.10 mOD); UNNAMED in the original log and no pencil seam correlation present. Sandstone/siltstone + black laminated mudstone roof; coaly mudstone + greenish grey weathered mudstone (seatearth position) below

intervals.verbatim: Black slightly weathered COALLY MUDSTONE-weak.Highly fractured
Greenish grey completely weathered MUDSTONE-very weak.Highly fractured
Greenish grey highly weathered MUDSTONE-moderately weak.Very closely and closely spaced fractures.Some carbonaceous material
annotations: END OF BOREHOLE at 37.40m (22.35 mOD). Greenish grey weathered mudstone occupies the seatearth/floor position beneath the coal, but is logged as MUDSTONE (not seatearth/fireclay), so classed mudstone

notes: Typed 1:25 borehole log, Wimpey Geotech, Lab Ref No S/26671; originator DAN, checked/approved LKR, dated 24/6/92; drilling commenced 11/6/92, completed by 16/6/92 (dates 12/6, 15/6, 16/6 appear in the date column). Client: Dept of Transport, A167 Durham Western Bypass Supplementary Ground Investigation. Boring: Cable Percussion to 25.60m; Rotary Core (PWF core barrel, foam flush) to 37.40m; Pilcon Wayfarer / HE36/80 tractor. Ground level 59.75 mOD; vertical. Log proper is 8 sheets = page images p01-p08 (interval numbering runs continuously across them). Page images p09-p12 are location/site plans by Roughton & Partners (Site Plan (1), (2), (3) + a general-layout sheet) with NO strata data; p11 and p12 are heavily toner-fogged photocopies but carry no log content. RECKONING: the log is internally arithmetic-consistent everywhere (every OD level equals 59.75 - depth); NO clerk slips found. Sequence: ~11m of drift (topsoil, clayey silty sands, glacial/laminated silty clays with coal-gravel and gravel, stiffening with depth) over a weathered SILTSTONE rockhead at ~24.70-25.60m, passing into Coal Measures siltstone, then sandstone (29.85-35.30m), then a coal-bearing sequence: interbedded sandstone/siltstone -> black laminated mudstone roof -> 1.10m COAL (35.55-36.65m) -> coaly + greenish-grey weathered mudstone floor, to base at 37.40m. The COAL is UNNAMED in the original and carries no pencil marginalia, so no seam name is asserted (seam_marks empty). Only handwriting on the whole log is a pencil 'Founding level ?' with an arrow on sheet 1 (~2.8m, an engineering note, recorded on interval 4). Groundwater: three seepages recorded in the completion remarks (2.60m rose 0.90m/15min, cut off by casing at 4.40m; 9.35m rose 1.95m/15min, cut off at 14.60m; 19.35m rose 1.05m/15min), recorded on the nearest intervals; borehole grouted on completion and a piezometer installed in an adjacent borehole under a lockable steel cover. Chiselling table (18.30-18.65m 1.00h; 20.85-21.80m 1.50h; 22.70-23.50m 2.00h; 24.25-25.25m 2.50h) recorded on interval 9. Final depth 37.40m matches the index depth 37.4m.
